H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: Pebble (n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence=strong) exceeds the overstimulation prediction (6.86) by ~0.3 points, indicating stronger than expected efficacy. 66% positive; rhythm praised as balanced/very settling. Settling typically around 10 minutes. YouTube reach is higher (30 views) but retention data are missing.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Pebble eases overstimulation better than expected, and listeners love the rhythm.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: Rainfall (n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, confidence=strong) aligns closely with the model’s overstimulation score (-0.06). 65% positive; rhythm perceived as very settling. Settling often at 10 minutes. YouTube: 5 views.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Rainfall calms sensory overload with a steady, soothing beat.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: Glacier (n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful) matches the predicted 6.86 (+0.18). 64% positive; balanced rhythm noted. Settlement split between 5‑10 minutes. YouTube: 8 views.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Glacier reliably settles overstimulated listeners within minutes.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: The Meadow protocol (n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong) outperforms the AutoGluon anxiety prediction of 7.84 by about 0.6 points, suggesting a modest under‑performance relative to model expectations. Distribution skews toward 7‑8 with 63% positive responses; qualitative data report 8‑10 ‘moderate to significant’ improvements and settle times around 10 minutes, especially for self‑listeners. Music and rhythm receive largely positive marks (balanced, very settling). YouTube reach is minimal (7 views, no retention data). The adviser source (5) hints that targeted routing may further improve outcomes.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Meadow helps most listeners feel noticeably calmer, especially when they listen on their own.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: Cottage recorded n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful, sitting ~0.9 points below the model’s anxiety score. Positive outcomes reach 67%, but music opinions are mixed, with five ‘neutral/so‑so’ remarks. Settling times cluster at ~5 minutes. No YouTube metrics beyond 6 views. Sources are all unknown, indicating limited adviser influence.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Cottage offers a moderate calm for anxiety, though some find the music neutral.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: Valley (n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful) is within 0.8 points of the predicted 7.84, showing reasonable alignment. Bimodal features are absent; 61% positive responses and a dominant ‘about 10 minutes’ settle time. Music feedback is split between liked and neutral. YouTube views are 4, with no retention data.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Valley provides steady relief with a typical 10‑minute settle time.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: Anchor (n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful) falls substantially below the model’s crisis prediction (7.96) by ~1.6 points, consistent with expected lower scores for acute distress. 53% report positive change, with many noting rapid settlement (5 minutes). Music reception is generally positive. YouTube reach: 6 views.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Anchor gives meaningful relief even in acute distress, though scores are modest.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: Harbor (n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful) also under‑performs the crisis model target, by ~1.4 points. Positive outcomes (55%) include several ‘significant improvement’ notes, though music opinions are mixed. Settlement occurs around 5‑10 minutes. YouTube: 4 views.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Harbor works for many in crisis, but responses vary more widely.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: Horizon (n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, confidence=meaningful) mirrors its peers with modest scores and a hint of bimodality (low‑score tail). 52% positive, rapid settlement reported. Music opinions range from liked to disliked. YouTube: 5 views.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Horizon shows mixed crisis outcomes, aligning with its design for rapid help.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: Lantern (n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, confidence=strong) is slightly below the sleep prediction (7.54) by ~0.35. 65% positive; rapid settlement (5 minutes) for many. Rhythm balanced; music largely liked. YouTube: 4 views.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Lantern supports sleep nicely, especially for those who need quick calm.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: Hammock (n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, confidence=meaningful) aligns within 0.5 of the sleep model. 67% positive; longer settle times (10‑20 minutes). Music feedback mixed. YouTube: 5 views.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Hammock offers a gentle bedtime aid, though some note a longer wind‑down.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: Midnight (n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful) sits ~0.5 below the predicted sleep score. 64% positive; settlement around 5‑10 minutes. Rhythm balanced; mixed music opinions. YouTube: 3 views.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Midnight works well for late‑night rest, even with fewer users.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: Compass (n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence=meaningful) trails the focus prediction (7.73) by >1 point, indicating room for improvement. 56% positive; many report ‘slightly busy’ rhythm. Music liked. YouTube: 5 views.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Compass gives modest focus help but can feel a bit busy.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: Waypoint (n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, confidence=meaningful) approaches the focus model score (−0.54). 66% positive; rhythm balanced; music loved. YouTube: 4 views.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Waypoint delivers clear focus benefits that many love.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: Summit (n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, confidence=meaningful) aligns closely with the focus prediction (−0.10). 71% positive; music highly praised; rhythm balanced. YouTube: 15 views, the highest in THRIVING.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Summit provides the strongest focus boost across the series.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: Ember (n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, confidence=meaningful) is modestly below the creative‑flow zone (no direct prediction) but aligns with its own observed spread. 53% positive; 6‑10 minute settle times. Rhythm balanced; music liked by most. YouTube: 2 views.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Ember modestly boosts creative flow for many listeners.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: Current (n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, confidence=meaningful) shows a slight uptick over L1. 58% positive; settlement around 5‑10 minutes. Rhythm balanced; music loved by many. YouTube: 2 views.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Current slightly improves creativity and is well‑received.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: Aurora (n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, confidence=developing) exceeds its peers, within 0.6 of the model’s creative‑flow estimate (not explicit). 67% positive; settlement ~10 minutes. Rhythm and music highly praised. YouTube: 7 views. Notable drift (sim=0.586).
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Aurora stands out as a surprisingly effective creative‑flow aid.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: Stone (n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, confidence=meaningful) sits ~0.65 below the maintenance prediction (7.69). 64% positive; settlement often 20 minutes. Rhythm balanced; modest YouTube (3 views). Drift noted (sim=0.565).
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Stone helps maintain mental balance, though its impact is modest.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: Axis (n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful) under‑performs the maintenance model by ~1 point and shows high variance. 59% positive; some report ‘busy’ rhythm. YouTube: 4 views. Drift (sim=0.637).
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Axis offers balanced maintenance but responses are mixed.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: Orbit (n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, confidence=developing) is ~0.76 below the maintenance prediction and exhibits pronounced drift (sim=0.557). Positive rate 71%; rhythm mixed. YouTube: 3 views.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Orbit shows variable maintenance benefits, needing more data.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: Breeze (n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful) is ~1.5 points below the low‑intensity prediction (8.23). 54% positive; rhythm balanced; music loved by many. YouTube: 8 views.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Breeze gives a mild mood lift, less than hoped for.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: Sunrise (n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful) remains ~1.5 below the model. 64% positive; rhythm balanced; music liked. YouTube: 8 views.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Sunrise offers a steady mood boost with balanced energy.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: Updraft (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing) is ~2 points under the low‑intensity prediction. 56% positive; rhythm mixed; music modestly liked. YouTube: 4 views.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Updraft delivers only modest mood improvement.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: Footpath (n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful) under‑performs the motivation prediction (7.88) by >1.5 points. 45% positive; rhythm balanced; music liked. YouTube: 5 views.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Footpath provides modest motivation, though many see limited change.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: Trail (n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing) remains below motivation target (‑1.67). 43% positive; rhythm mixed; music loved by some. YouTube: 7 views.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Trail shows modest drive benefits with mixed listener feedback.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: Switchback (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing) exceeds its motivation prediction by ~‑0.72, showing an unexpected high mean despite small n. 62% positive; rhythm balanced; music liked. YouTube: 7 views.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Switchback surprisingly lifts motivation for a few, but results vary.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: Cavern (n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful) falls ~0.79 below the mind‑clarity prediction (7.54). 54% positive; rhythm very settling; music loved. YouTube: 8 views.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Cavern improves mental clarity for many, with a soothing rhythm.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: Starlight (n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing) is ~0.73 under the mind‑clarity model. 56% positive; rapid settlement; music loved. YouTube: 12 views.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Starlight gives a gentle clarity boost and quick settle.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: Cosmos (n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, confidence=developing) sits far below the mind‑clarity prediction (‑1.84) with a clearly bimodal distribution (scores 0‑4 and 8‑10). 50% positive; mixed music feedback. YouTube: 8 views. Strong signal for subgroup analysis.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Cosmos works well for some, poorly for others – a split response.

sphere_commentary.overview:
HEALING protocols show the strongest and most consistent outcomes, while THRIVING delivers high peaks and TRANSFORMING displays greater variability.
• HEALING mean≈7.0 with low variance across 12 protocols.
• THRIVING peaks at 7.63 (Focus) but shows mixed spread.
• TRANSFORMING averages 6.6, with several low‑mean and bimodal entries.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Efficacy patterns align with each series’ design intent.
• HEALING excels in stress, sleep, and overstimulation regulation.
• THRIVING’s HOLD arcs yield high Focus scores and moderate Creativity.
• TRANSFORMING’s ASCENT arcs produce wider outcome spread and subgroup signals.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
A few protocols diverge from series trends and merit deeper inspection.
• H-CR-L3 shows modest mean (6.52) with a hint of bimodality.
• X-MC-L3 (Cosmos) displays a low mean (5.70) and high std (2.45).
• T-MB-L3 exhibits notable drift (sim=0.557) suggesting unstable response patterns.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Targeted tweaks could sharpen efficacy signals.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H-AX-L1 – adjust Adviser routing to prioritize self‑listeners with faster settle times.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H-OS-L1 – boost promotional routing to increase YouTube reach (currently 7 views).
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MC-L3 – create an L2 variant or subgroup split to address low mean and high variance.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T-MB-L3 – gather larger sample and re‑evaluate drift; consider stabilizing protocol parameters.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T-FC-L3 – experiment with an extended hold phase to test if higher mean sustains.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding drift and clustering reveal evolving model interpretations.
• CROSS‑RUN DRIFT: T-MB-L3, T-MB-L1, T-CF-L3 show strong drift (<0.6 similarity).
• SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: T-MB-L2 ↔ T-MB-L3 (sim=0.844) form a tight analytic core; T-CF-L3 remains isolated.
• Confidence: drift metrics based on few runs; interpret with caution.
